definition: Partition a large training corpus across shards, build local vector indexes such as HNSW, route each evaluation query to a subset of partitions, and merge local top-k results into SPECTRA pairwise edges.

scale_profile.best_for: Vector similarity audits whose train corpus exceeds one-machine memory or latency targets.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Cluster-scale vector collections where shard routing and recall monitoring are available.
scale_profile.avoid_when: The project cannot measure recall or ensure deterministic query/index versions.

tunable_parameters.name: num_partitions
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls index size and routing granularity.

quality_gates: Measure global recall@k against exact or less-partitioned search on a subset.
Report partition routing rule and number of queried partitions.
Record index versioning because SPECTRA curves should be reproducible.
failure_modes: Shard routing can miss true neighbors in unqueried partitions.
Uneven partitions can create load imbalance and unstable latency.
Index updates can change pairwise edges between audit runs.
example_usage: Use dense_vector_topk_similarity.py for a single-machine exact contract; distributed ANN systems should write the same pairwise_similarity.csv schema after merging shard results.

citations.title: Pyramid: A general framework for distributed similarity search
citations.evidence: Describes distributed similarity search with HNSW, partitioning, selective partition queries, and throughput/quality tradeoffs.

citations.title: Approximate nearest neighbor graph construction and search for biological data
citations.evidence: Applies approximate nearest-neighbor graph/search ideas to large biological data settings.
